sites.name: Glomfjord
sites.desc_en: Hydropower-rich coastal site, 12.5 MW modules to 50 MW+ campus.
sites.desc_no: Vannkraftrik kystlokasjon, 12,5 MW-moduler skalerer til 50 MW+ campus.

sites.name: Bakki
sites.desc_en: Geothermal baseload, 50 MW initial 2027, scaling to 200 MW.
sites.desc_no: Geotermisk grunnlast, 50 MW oppstart 2027, skalerer til 200 MW.
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Dispose — develop if land and power can be secured near term; otherwise sell if a material offer is obtained from Meralyte or others.
sites.nbs_assessment: Support from PM's office and municipality. Land secured by MOU; lease under negotiation. Subsea cables being renewed with new connections to North America. Draft PPA terms received but utility/TSO want end-user interest before definitive contracts.
sites.project_description: Greenfield plot adjoining the (now ceased) PCC Silicon plant. Mostly flat, no trees, small stream to one side. Old pre-fabricated office buildings on part of the site. Substation across the road on the site boundary.
sites.size_ha: Parcel A: 5; Parcel B: 15 (more adjoining parcels available)
sites.exact_power_status: Under LOI with utility. Power & transmission available subject to PPA.
sites.fibre_strands: Dark fibre available (via FARICE extension); dedicated strands possible under future subsea scenario; allocation TBC.
sites.fibre_general_info: One of the strongest-connected sites in the Nordics. Three independent subsea systems land in Iceland (FARICE, DANICE, IRIS) with direct routes to UK, mainland Europe and North America. Systems being renewed and expanded with new/upgraded NA connectivity. Multiple technical and commercial discussions with FARICE via JLL.
sites.latency_summary: Dublin ~13.4 ms · Slough UK ~15.5–19.9 ms · Frankfurt ~19.9 ms · Amsterdam ~19.7 ms · Oslo ~22.2 ms · Paris ~22.7 ms · NY ~42.8 ms (IRIS).
sites.zoning_status: Parcel A zoned (one plot complete); Parcel B (expansion) zoning to be conducted by the municipality (~12 months estimated).
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Power procurement — Landsvirkjun's new process requires an end user prior to MOU signing (currently mitigated by an LOI from Solstice Data).
sites.current_control: Lease negotiations (extension to MOU agreed Feb-26)
sites.planned_ultimate_control: Effective freehold (long leasehold; freehold not possible in Iceland)

sites.name: Skibotn
sites.desc_en: Sub-Arctic Norwegian site with hydropower headroom and free-air cooling at 69° N.
sites.desc_no: Sub-arktisk norsk lokasjon med vannkraftkapasitet og frikjøling på 69° N.
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Mining or Dispose — shovel-ready in medium term (Q1-27) subject to power. Only 15 MW so possibly more suited to mining; alternatively landowner not opposed to bitcoin mining.
sites.nbs_assessment: Secure but small. Close to new Arctic fibre project linking North Norway to North America/Canada/Japan. Municipality investigating concession power (2-3 MW est., not yet included). No employment tax in municipality.
sites.project_description: Identified by the Scale42 team in 2023 as a strong data-centre candidate at the Norwegian landing point of the Far North Fibre project. Backed by both the current landowner and the municipality. Brownfield site with existing industrial buildings; surrounding fjord sparsely populated with natural woodland. Greenfield development possible on areas within the plot.
sites.exact_power_status: In queue, awaiting allocation. 1st in local DSO queue, 2nd in regional TSO queue.
sites.heat_reuse: Municipal support for study engaging local businesses.
sites.fibre_general_info: Carrier-diverse terrestrial fibre via the northern Norwegian backbone, with southbound routing into Sweden and continental Europe.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~10–15 ms · Stockholm ~15–20 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms. Subsea: indirect via Sweden/Denmark (DANICE, IRIS).
sites.other_access_notes: Close proximity to Far North Fibre project.
sites.zoning_status: Plot zoned but not for data centre. Strong municipal political support; special dispensation granted (expires Q3 2028). Detailed zoning expected within 12 months.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Mayor and landowner need consistent direction on local engagement.
sites.contractual_arrangement: Roald Normann Consulting (land agent). Ground works included within land purchase.

sites.name: Reykjavík
sites.desc_en: Greater Reykjavík capacity adjacent to existing fibre — low-latency to North America.
sites.desc_no: Kapasitet i Stor-Reykjavík ved siden av eksisterende fiber — lav latency til Nord-Amerika.
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Dispose.
sites.nbs_assessment: GIGA-42 lead for powered land supplied by Reykjavík Energy (RE) in Municipality of Ölfus. Commercial discussions with RE underway; GIGA-42 well positioned.
sites.project_description: Early discussion with ON Power (Reykjavík Energy subsidiary) to acquire land and power near Reykjavík. 70,000 sqm available, fully zoned for DC; additional 210,000 sqm available. Power and land both owned by ON Power. Geothermal direct from Hellisheiði — no grid connection agreement required. Heat-reuse opportunities within the geothermal park.
sites.exact_power_status: In negotiations with utility. Power & transmission understood available subject to PPA.
sites.heat_reuse: Industrial park has existing tenants that could take heat (e.g. direct-air carbon capture).
sites.fibre_general_info: Fastest UK connection within Iceland; closest DC to FARICE cable.
sites.latency_summary: London ~15–20 ms · Amsterdam ~18–25 ms · Frankfurt ~20–28 ms · Copenhagen ~20–30 ms · NY ~40–45 ms. Direct access to multiple Icelandic subsea systems (IRIS, DANICE, FARICE-class). Strong resilience compared to other Arctic sites.
sites.zoning_status: Parcel A fully zoned for DC; Parcel B (expansion) requires further zoning.
sites.current_control: Energy subsidiary (RE) — materially reducing execution risk

sites.name: Kristinestad
sites.desc_en: Coastal Finnish site at the wind-power heartland with deep grid capacity.
sites.desc_no: Finsk kystlokasjon i vindkraftens kjerneområde med stor nettkapasitet.
sites.category_rationale: Develop — shovel-ready in ~12 months with ~500 MW of potential consumption. First 60 MW (6-9 mo); next 40 MW (12-24 mo); above 100 MW via 400 kV (24-36 mo).
sites.nbs_assessment: Municipality stated intent to issue a reservation agreement Feb 2026 — after which project can commence in earnest.
sites.project_description: Brought to Scale42 March 2025. Out-bid other developers for a 500 MW+ facility. Strong municipal backing through extensive stakeholder engagement and multiple site visits. Large expanse of dense woodland with undulating topography, situated next to a main highway.
sites.restrictions: No Chinese/Russian end customers/funders. Prefer no Israeli funders or end customers. Do not want APL UK Ltd as part of project.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: GlobalConnect brief report indicates fibre connectivity sufficient. Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €385k, MRC €6,180/mo.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.airport_access: 1 hr to domestic; 4 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Reservation agreement out for municipal signature (Feb 2026). Zoning approval targeted Nov/Dec 2026.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Reservation agreement carries KPIs Scale42 must meet to progress to purchase/leasehold (Q1 2027); KPIs are easily met.
sites.current_control: Reservation agreement out for municipal signature (Feb-11)
sites.contractual_arrangement: DLA Piper Finland; Peter Sladey; Permitting: Ramboll, AINS, Granlund.

sites.name: Nivala
sites.desc_en: North Ostrobothnia site with strong onshore wind and existing 110 kV connection.
sites.desc_no: Lokasjon i Nord-Österbotten med sterk landvind og eksisterende 110 kV-tilknytning.
sites.category_rationale: Develop* or Dispose — shovel-ready in near term.
sites.nbs_assessment: Reservation negotiations with municipality. Scale42 LOI submitted to secure negotiations. Backing for removal of nearby road-bound airstrip.
sites.project_description: Identified by Scale42 October 2025. LOI submitted Jan 2026 for plot near a 400 kV substation; potential to grow to >500 MW. Flat land with slight undulation, mostly managed woodland.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Within Finland's national fibre network; site-specific connectivity TBC. Regional utility fibre with access to long-haul routes south.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.zoning_status: Outline zoning required. Industrial zoning (with DC permission) expected by Q4 2026. Sweco commissioned for environmental study.
sites.contractual_arrangement: DLA Piper Finland; Permitting: Ramboll, AINS, Granlund.

sites.name: Jokkmokk
sites.desc_en: Lapland hydropower belt — co-located with Sweden's largest hydropower production region.
sites.desc_no: Vannkraftbeltet i Lappland — samlokalisert med Sveriges største vannkraftregion.
sites.bid_status: Lead *hot* (was Project, reclassified after discussion with Luke)
sites.category_rationale: Develop — shovel-ready <12 months with 60 MW RFS in 18-21 months. Near central Finnish (sic — Swedish) power corridor; strategically located for long-term expansion.
sites.nbs_assessment: Out-bid 5 other developers; partnership with Jokkmokk municipality near a 446 MW hydro dam.
sites.project_description: Brought to Scale42 October 2025 by the municipality. Through stakeholder engagement and capability demos, Scale42 out-bid 5 other developers. Partnership negotiations underway. Large undulating plot mostly native woodland near the Miessaure hydroelectric dam.
sites.exact_power_status: Available capacity confirmed in email correspondence with Vattenfall
sites.fibre_general_info: Strong Swedish long-haul corridor. Multiple north–south backbones through Norrbotten with onward breakout to Stockholm and the European subsea/terrestrial systems. Genuine carrier diversity possible. DWDM scalability (400G class).
sites.latency_summary: Stockholm ~8–12 ms · Frankfurt ~20–25 ms · Amsterdam ~20–25 ms · London ~25–30 ms.
sites.other_access_notes: Close proximity to multiple hydro opportunities.
sites.zoning_status: Working with municipality on landplot identification and zoning strategy. Zoning consultant to be engaged.
sites.contractual_arrangement: DLA Piper Finland; Permitting: Ramboll, AINS, Granlund.

sites.name: Kronoby
sites.desc_en: Bilingual coastal Finnish site with wind and hydro complementary supply.
sites.desc_no: Tospråklig finsk kystlokasjon med komplementær vind- og vannkraftforsyning.
sites.category_rationale: Develop — shovel-ready in near term subject to validating municipal power infrastructure timelines.
sites.nbs_assessment: Currently negotiating lease agreement with Kronoby municipality to reserve the site.
sites.project_description: Identified by Peter Sladey November 2025. West coast of Finland, just south of Kokkola (regional hub). Engaging with municipality to reserve plot during general then detailed zoning. Flat coastal land, mix of agricultural and forestry use.
sites.restrictions: No Chinese/Russian end customers / funders. No crypto.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €395k, MRC €6,780/mo.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.zoning_status: Detailed planning expected to finish summer 2026.

sites.name: Torsboda
sites.bid_status: Lead *hot* (was Project, changed by TB & WT 19/02)
sites.category_rationale: Close / Dispose — shovel-ready 2028 with ~400 MW potential. Power connection 2029-2030 so RFS much longer. Strategic location with proximity to multiple hydropower projects.
sites.nbs_assessment: Bid submitted Jan 2026 against several other DC companies. Result expected Feb 2026. Early feedback indicated front-runner status.
sites.project_description: Brought to Scale42 January 2026 by Peter Sladey. Zoned land near Sundsvall, SE2 Sweden, with ~1,200 MW of power and an electrification timeline. ~15% slope on the westward side of a hill; managed woodland cleared for industrial use.
sites.exact_power_status: Power becomes available and allocated to site owner in 2028.
sites.fibre_general_info: Strategic central-eastern Sweden location with dense carrier presence and short routes to continental Europe. Strong for latency-sensitive workloads.
sites.latency_summary: Stockholm ~5–8 ms · Frankfurt ~18–22 ms · Amsterdam ~18–22 ms · London ~22–25 ms.
sites.zoning_status: Already zoned (190 ha, 30% building rights). Power connection target 2027.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Currently in competition with other bidders; Scale42 has strong municipal track record.

sites.name: Liperi
sites.category_rationale: Develop — can be shovel-ready in near term.
sites.nbs_assessment: Very secure, in later stages of lease negotiations.
sites.project_description: Located just outside Joensuu, a regional hub for eastern/central Finland. The Liperi project, identified February 2025, is being established in tandem with the Kitee project with the full backing of Liperi municipality and Business Joensuu. Land: very flat with sporadic woodland close to existing industrial zone, main road adjacent.
sites.size_ha: Parcel A: 9 ha (2+7 lease); Parcel B: 23 ha
sites.restrictions: Verbal client/funding restrictions: no Chinese or Russian end customers / funders.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.heat_reuse: Planned — local offtakers available, district heating demand identified.
sites.cooling: Air — perimeter cooling; DLC — room-level CDU
sites.fibre_general_info: Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €195k, MRC €6,180/mo, 36-month term. Permitting and warm-month build (~6 months).
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · Paris ~35 ms · New York ~95–105 ms (via Helsinki: Sea Lion to Germany, two cables to Sweden, Northern Cable under construction).
sites.airport_access: 2 km to domestic; 4 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Parcel A zoned. Parcel B requires zoning (consultant-led).
sites.planning_status: Building rights 50% (raisable to 70% via deviation permit, ~4 weeks). Building / environmental / emissions / water permits expected ~12 weeks.
sites.contractual_arrangement: DLA Piper Finland (legal); Peter Sladey (land agent); Permitting consultants: Ramboll, AINS, Granlund.

sites.name: Kitee
sites.category_rationale: Develop — can be shovel-ready in near term.
sites.nbs_assessment: Secured by way of lease. Just needs capital to progress.
sites.project_description: Identified March 2025, located on the Puhos industrial park. 50 MW data centre with on-ward expansion potential, fully backed by Kitee municipality and Business Joensuu. Undulating topography on a moraine deposit currently being excavated.
sites.restrictions: No Chinese/Russian end customers or funders. Do not want APL Europe (UK) Ltd as part of project.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Municipality confirmed no fibre availability concerns. Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €310k, MRC €6,180/mo.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · Paris ~35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.airport_access: 1 hr to domestic; 4 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Already zoned, building rights 50% (raisable to 70% via deviation permit, ~4 weeks per Mayor).
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Although low risk, proximity to Russian border may deter some clients.
sites.contractual_arrangement: DLA Piper Finland; CFE (electrical engineering & infrastructure study); Peter Sladey (land agent); Permitting: Ramboll, AINS, Granlund.

sites.name: Sørfold
sites.category_rationale: Dispose — shovel-ready in near term. Power connection 24–36 months per Ramboll report — not a near-term development site.
sites.nbs_assessment: Zoning has faced political challenges due to old master plan, but accelerating since Nov 2025. Majority of municipal political parties voting in favour. Working with administration before detailed regulation of the landplot.
sites.project_description: Identified by Scale42 in 2021; in zoning approval process since identification and land option. Undulating greenfield site in the centre of a valley with a mix of grassland and natural woodland. "50 MW allocated; 50 MW+ expansion potential".
sites.exact_power_status: Allocated and available
sites.fibre_general_info: Served via Nordlandsnett with access to Telenor, Telia and GlobalConnect. Strong for northern Norway; final allocation and route diversity to be locked down. International reach indirect via Sweden/Denmark into DANICE / IRIS — not a cable-landing location.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~8–12 ms · Stockholm ~10–15 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · Amsterdam ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms.
sites.other_access_notes: 5 km to commercial port. Multiple data-centre projects ongoing in area; NATO establishing offices in nearby city.
sites.zoning_status: Zoning being changed from LNF to industry (with data centre) with political support. Working with ABO Plan & Arkitektur on detailed zoning. Permits expected to be approved at or shortly after zoning.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Re-zoning requires more political capital than other regions; current political will is good.
sites.current_control: Secure option agreement with landowner; full TSO 50 MW allocation

sites.name: Alajärvi
sites.category_rationale: Dispose / Close / Mining — shovel-ready in ~24 months with >500 MW potential consumption. Adjacent to main western Finnish power corridor.
sites.nbs_assessment: Scoping. Reservation > lease > purchase will be the process.
sites.project_description: Identified by Scale42 October 2025. Scoping plots around the town with strong municipal support. Land still being scoped.
sites.dso: Elenia Verkko Oyj & EPV Energia Oy
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Within Finland's national fibre footprint via regional utility fibre. Specific carrier options and physical route diversity not confirmed; targeted fibre DD required.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.zoning_status: No zoning in place. After reservation: general (area plan) → detailed zoning, 18-24 months. Green Transition Placement Permit could accelerate.
sites.contractual_arrangement: DLA Piper Finland; Permitting: Ramboll, AINS, Granlund.

sites.name: Robertsfors
sites.category_rationale: Dispose / Close / Mining — shovel-ready in 24 months, >800 MW potential. Near northern Finnish (sic — Swedish) power-house, strategically located for long-term expansion.
sites.nbs_assessment: Project introduced by JLL into GIGA-42; could present a good project in future.
sites.project_description: Brought to GIGA-42 by JLL. Sale of land by Holmen. Viewed as a follow-on for other Swedish projects re: time to powered land. Land near a main highway outside Robertsfors with a mix of open grassland and forestry.
sites.exact_power_status: Phased delivery starting 2029 via Skellefteå Kraft.
sites.fibre_general_info: Strong regional fibre with access to Sweden's national backbone; suitable for scalable AI and cloud deployments.
sites.latency_summary: Stockholm ~10–12 ms · Frankfurt ~22–26 ms · Amsterdam ~22–26 ms · London ~26–30 ms.

sites.name: Nuuk
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Dispose — long lead, but strategic: (i) placement between Europe, NA & Pacific; (ii) commercial foothold in Greenland / expanded company footprint; (iii) media coverage for development.
sites.nbs_assessment: Early-stage discussions with municipality keen to locate a DC near Nuuk airport. Municipality very keen to involve Scale42 due to Glomfjord precedent. Hydropower expansion planned to increase availability.
sites.project_description: Brought to GIGA-42 JV for exploration of building a DC near Nuuk airport, Greenland. Greenland government desiring to move away from North American businesses. Verbal confirmation from senior NunaGreen business developer that GIGA-42 are preferred partners (Glomfjord track record cited). Exposed rock and undulated topography adjacent to Nuuk airport.
sites.exact_power_status: Not verified
sites.fibre_general_info: Nuuk's fibre is a limited but strategic subsea footprint with national coverage and international access via Iceland. Effectively single-region from a fibre perspective. Best suited to sovereign compute, research, government, edge or specialised workloads where jurisdiction/climate/energy matter more than ultra-low latency.
sites.latency_summary: Reykjavik ~15–20 ms · Copenhagen ~35–45 ms · London ~45–55 ms · Frankfurt ~50–60 ms · NY ~40–50 ms. Resilience depends on Greenland–Iceland corridor and downstream routing.
sites.zoning_status: Status of planning, zoning and permits TBC; not perceived as risk due to long power timeline.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Although low, geo-political risks to Greenland projects.

sites.name: Tjeldbergodden
sites.category_rationale: Develop or Mining or Close — does not meet 18-month RFS criterion (more like 30 months). Existing heat-offtaker (DC heat for adjacent fish farm). Strong ESG/media angle. Potential mining play if heat off-take enables substantial power savings (Eltax exemption).
sites.nbs_assessment: Early-stage discussions with major planned aquaculture development. Scale42 would take surplus power and land for DC; waste heat to fish farm. Formal agreements still to complete.
sites.project_description: Early-stage discussions with Salfjord AS, a local aquaculture business exploring waste heat coupling for salmon farming. Located next to the sea half-way up the Norwegian coast, sloping topography on exposed rocky base.
sites.restrictions: Must provision heat supply to neighbouring aquaculture producer "Salfjord" as a project condition.
sites.exact_power_status: Power application submitted via project partner
sites.fibre_strands: Dark fibre available, landing point next to site
sites.fibre_general_info: Tjeldbergodden is served by Norway's national carrier ecosystem with high-capacity services available. Proximity to industrial infrastructure and transport corridors provides strong ducting and last-mile options. Final carrier selection and physical path diversity TBC.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~5–10 ms · Stockholm ~10–18 ms · Copenhagen ~12–20 ms · Frankfurt ~20–30 ms · Amsterdam ~20–30 ms · London ~25–35 ms. Indirect via Norwegian/Danish/UK subsea systems with strong European breakout.
sites.airport_access: 1.2 hr to domestic (KSU); 2 hr to domestic w/ international connections (TRD)
sites.college_university: 1.2 hr to college; 2 hr to university
sites.zoning_status: Detailed zoning plan submitted to Aure Municipality for 142.5 daa across gnr. 51, bnr. 1/6/44 (industry / data centre). Planning expected to complete early 2027.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Competition emerging via on-going negotiations with Salfjord AS; Scale42 has strong track-record of out-competing other DC entities.

sites.name: Honningsvåg
sites.category_rationale: Mining or Dispose — small (15 MW) far-north Norway. Best mining location in the Nordics: no electricity tax, lowest power price in Europe (avg 8.71 EUR/MWh in 2025), no employment tax, municipality and landowners open to any form of employment incl. mining.
sites.nbs_assessment: Secure but small (15 MW) far north of Norway. Power 8.71 EUR/MWh in 2025 incl. taxes. Lower employment tax in municipality.
sites.project_description: Identified by Scale42 in 2023; potentially the second-most-northerly data centre globally (after Prudhoe Bay, Alaska). Rough rocky arctic terrain on the outskirts of Honningsvåg near the airport.
sites.exact_power_status: In queue, awaiting allocation. 1st in local DSO queue.
sites.heat_reuse: Municipal support for study engaging local businesses.
sites.fibre_general_info: Local fibre served via regional utility (Area Nett); specific named carrier beyond utility not confirmed.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~15–20 ms · Stockholm ~20–25 ms · Frankfurt ~30–35 ms · London ~35–40 ms. Subsea: indirect via southern Norway / Sweden.
sites.college_university: 15 min to college; domestic flight to university
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Workforce — population ~3,000, but Scale42 has trained data-centre teams in similarly small communities (Glomfjord, pop ~2,000, 30 MW).

sites.name: Kemi
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Close / Mining — near-term shovel-ready (~45 MW) with 1000+ MW long-term. Complex brownfield. Acquisition needs to be attractive. 70 MW currently allocated to a bitcoin miner; if unlocked, ~115 MW near-term.
sites.nbs_assessment: Advanced acquisition stage. One of a small number of buyers invited to third stage of buyer selection / DD. Project securable with capital. Possible +70 MW starting capacity (subject to negotiations).
sites.project_description: Identified by Peter Sladey March 2025. Brownfield industrial island with own power infrastructure, potential to draw ~1 GW (with upgrades). Existing tenants and heavy-set industrial buildings. Two points of entry to the island. Flat industrial island in northern Finland.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Existing fibre sufficient for data centre; needs build to site (~1.5 km east). Possible private network in old paper mill area. Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €255k, MRC €8,020/mo.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.airport_access: 10 km to domestic; 2 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Already zoned; 190 ha site with 30% building rights.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Currently in competition with other bidders for the industrial site; Scale42 has municipal backing.

sites.name: Mikkeli
sites.category_rationale: Develop — landplot secured by reservation. Ready to commence permits. Achieving max power likely requires capital or off-take commitment to DSO; otherwise capacity capped ~50 MW.
sites.nbs_assessment: Outline zoning in place; progressing detailed regulation. Permits can run in parallel. DSO can install new substation breakers once permits are in. "Good site in my opinion."
sites.project_description: Brought to Scale42 March 2025 to develop a data centre just outside Mikkeli, a regional and rapidly expanding hub in central Finland. Heavy engagement with both the municipality and the local DSO. Flat arable/forestry mix with ~2 m topographic differences.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits & DSO agreement
sites.fibre_general_info: Telia 400G DWDM: insufficient DWDM capacity for this connection currently.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.airport_access: 10 km to domestic; 2 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Reservation agreement signed. Zoning approval target Nov/Dec 2026.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Two additional data-centre operators planning in Mikkeli — possible competition for power and labour. Project may be delayed by DSO grid-works approvals.

sites.name: Boden
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Close — 'IF' Stegra goes bankrupt, immediately zoned land & access to power, shovel-ready within 12 months.
sites.nbs_assessment: Early-stage discussions; waiting on confirmation that the existing development is a no-go (Stegra moving toward bankruptcy).
sites.project_description: Prospective project following the bankruptcy of Stegra (hydrogen manufacturer). Potential to free ~1 GW of power. Brownfield industrial (not fully constructed).
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to bankruptcy of Stegra
sites.fibre_strands: Available via national backbone (dark fibre possible)
sites.fibre_general_info: Mature hyperscale cluster with strong redundancy, multiple long-haul routes and carrier diversity. Subsea exposure indirect via southern Sweden → Denmark (DANICE / IRIS).
sites.latency_summary: Stockholm ~10–12 ms · Frankfurt ~22–26 ms · Amsterdam ~22–26 ms · London ~26–30 ms.
sites.zoning_status: Already zoned; awaiting sale by entity in administration.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Project depends on insolvency of Stegra (hydrogen producer).

sites.name: Hadsel
sites.category_rationale: Close — not suitable for mining; no land yet identified.
sites.nbs_assessment: Substation identified with power. No land identified — focus has been on other projects. Classified as "lead".
sites.project_description: Substation identified with power; no land identified yet. Lead-stage only.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to land identification, application & allocation
sites.fibre_general_info: Terrestrial fibre with potential dual-route redundancy through northern and southern Norwegian paths.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~10–15 ms · Stockholm ~15–20 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms.

sites.name: Sortland
sites.category_rationale: Close — not suitable for mining; no land yet identified.
sites.nbs_assessment: Substation identified with power. No land identified — focus has been on other projects. Classified as "lead".
sites.project_description: Substation identified with power; no land identified yet. Lead-stage only.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to land identification, application & allocation
sites.fibre_general_info: Well-connected regional hub with resilient terrestrial fibre feeding national and international backbones.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~10–15 ms · Stockholm ~15–20 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms.

sites.name: Salsbruket
sites.target_mw: 5 MW (possibly 80 MW+ subject to substation power)
sites.category_rationale: Mining or Close — smaller opportunity from a hydropower producer. Land adjacent to power plant and a fish farm requiring heat. Not progressed due to small capacity (5 MW) but remains as a lead.
sites.nbs_assessment: Smaller opportunity from hydropower producer. Land adjacent to power plant and fish farm requiring heat. Remains a lead.
sites.project_description: Behind-the-meter power supply from a hydropower plant owned by Albert Collett. Potential delivery of waste heat to a local MOWI fish farm. No power application required for behind-the-meter supply. Additional power potentially via existing 66 kV / 22 kV substation. Good water access for cooling. Proof-of-concept before access to Collett's wider hydro portfolio.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to agreement with landowner who is also the utility
sites.fibre_general_info: Central Norway location with access to national fibre routes; final connectivity dependent on confirmed site selection.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~8–12 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms.
sites.airport_access: 1 hr to domestic; 4 hr to domestic w/ international connections (TRD); two airports in between
sites.college_university: 1 hr to university; 2.5 hr to university

sites.name: Rovaniemi
sites.category_rationale: Dispose / Close / Mining — shovel-ready in 24 months, >800 MW potential. Near central Finnish power corridor; strategic for long-term expansion.
sites.nbs_assessment: Early-stage; site explored June 2025 then halted, awaiting bandwidth to chase again with hydropower operator Kemijoki Oy.
sites.project_description: Identified April 2025 near the Pirtikoski hydrodam alongside Business Rovaniemi. Discussions halted in expectation of finding a more workforce-friendly location. Gently sloping site with sporadic woodland and multiple access roads, adjacent to 400 kV substation and hydropower dam.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Rovaniemi sits on Finland's national backbone with Elisa and Telia present, dark fibre available subject to strand allocation. Routes south toward Helsinki for international subsea breakout. Site-specific routing and commercial terms to be validated upon reactivation.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.

sites.name: Varkaus
sites.desc_en: Industrial-ready Finnish site with strong municipal backing.
sites.desc_no: Industriklar finsk lokasjon med sterk kommunal støtte.
